headline: Hello 2025 5 unique planners to help you map out the year ahead  GMA News Online
text:
New year, new plans!
Using a year planner to map out your days ahead can be beneficial if you want to start organizing your goals, daily chores, journals, and other plans for the new year.
Knowing what you have to deal with daily gives you the power to feel in control of your schedule, helping you prioritize what's most important for the days, weeks, and months to come.
Just in case you don't have a planner yet for 2025, here are five picks curated by the GMA News Online Lifestyle team.
1. Muji Philippines
A post shared by MUJI Philippines (@muji_ph)
Muji Philippines offers several types of planners, may it be dated or with bare pages. The Muji 2025 Monthly Weekly View-At-Once Planner lets you see monthly, daily, and vertical monthly plans at a glance.
2. Limelight Philippines
A post shared by Limelight (@limelight.ph)
Limelight Philippines's 2025 planner collection utilizes one of the most popular personality assessment tools, the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI). Each planner is designed to inspire self-awareness through daily journaling, offering organization and personal growth.
3. Belle De Jour
A post shared by Belle De Jour Power Planner (@ilovebdj)
The 2025 Belle De Jour planner by Darlyn Ty-Nilo contains pages with beautiful affirmations reminding you that you can reach your dreams and other empowering messages to help you embrace your best year yet.
4. Starbucks Philippines
A post shared by Starbucks Philippines (@starbucksph)
If you're an avid Starbucks planner user, you have until January 9 to collect 18 stickers to claim a planner for free.
The 2025 Starbucks Traditions planner comes in a black hardbound with gold accents. It has a two-toned leather pouch in blue and cream with a rose gold magnetic lock featuring Starbucks's iconic siren symbol, as well as a black-inked rose gold pen attached to the planner.
5. Pickup Coffee
A post shared by PICKUP COFFEE (@pickupcoffeeph)
Pickup Coffee collaborated with Winnie Wong to produce a 2025 planner, which you can claim for free when you finish collecting 35 digital stamps through the Pickup Coffee app. The promo is valid until supplies last.
Aside from the monthly and weekly undated calendar, the planner consists of a page for goals, a to-do list, a monthly budget, a tracker for monthly expenses, and journal entries.
—MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: Visitors spend New Year’s Day at Luneta  GMA News Online
text:
Visitors flocked to the Luneta Park in Manila to celebrate the first day of the year with their families.
According to a Balitanghali report by Ivan Mayrina on Wednesday, the occasional rains and cloudy skies did not stop visitors from enjoying the scenery offered by the famous park.
Some visitors took advantage of the New Year’s holiday to spend a relaxing time off with their families or loved ones.
“May bonding po kami, masaya,” said a visitor.
One of the key features visitors enjoyed were the wide-open spaces, which were popular to the visiting children, while loudspeakers played Christmas songs throughout the park.
A light and sound show is expected in the evening.
The park is open until 10:00 p.m. —Jiselle Anne Casucian/RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: California attorney-general reminds immigrants of rights ahead of Trump inauguration  GMA News Online
text:
The Office of the Attorney General of California headed by Filipino-American Attorney General Rob Bonta has issued a consumer alert to inform California immigrants about their rights and protections under the law.
This came following US President-elect Donald Trump’s recent remarks about the arrest, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants.
“State and local law enforcement cannot ask for your immigration status for immigration enforcement purposes,” the alert read.
“California law expressly prohibits law enforcement from inquiring about a person’s immigration status for immigration enforcement purposes,” it added.
The alert said that state and local enforcement cannot assist the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) with immigration enforcement, with limited exceptions.
“This means state and local law enforcement cannot investigate, cannot interrogate, cannot arrest, and cannot detain you unless they are part of a joint federal task force where the primary purpose of the task force is not immigration enforcement,” it said.
State and local law enforcement also cannot share your personal information with immigration enforcement. —NB, GMA Integrated News

headline: 33 road accidents recorded amid New Year festivities  GMA News Online
text:
The Department of Health (DOH) said Wednesday it logged 33 new road traffic incidents amid the New Year revelry, bringing the total caseload this holiday season to 529.
The 529 cases recorded from December 22, 2024 until 6 a.m. of January 1, 2025 were 31% higher compared to the cases recorded during the same period last year.
Six people were killed this holiday season due to road crashes, four of whom died due to motorcycle accidents.
For those hitting the road after the holidays, the DOH reminded them to always wear a helmet when riding a motorcycle and use a seatbelt when driving or riding a car.
The agency also advised not to drive when intoxicated, always follow speed limits and road signs, and ensure that drivers have 7-8 hours of sleep before hitting the road.
Motorists who encounter road accidents may call 911 emergency hotline or 1555 DOH emergency hotline. —RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: PH to field 20 athletes to 2025 Asian Winter Games  GMA News Online
text:
The Philippines will field 20 athletes to the 2025 Asian Winter Games in Harbin, China this February.
According to the Philippine Olympic Committee, the country will compete in six of the 11 sports in the Harbin games.
The Curling Winter Sports Association of the Philippines or Curling Pilipinas will have 10 athletes for its team, with the whole delegation including its officers composed of Marc Angelo Pfister, Enrico Gabriel Pfister, Christian Patrick Haller, Alan Beat Frei, Jessica Pfister, Benjo Delarmente, Kathleen Dubberstein, Leilani Dubberstein, Sheila Mariano and Anne Marie Bonache.
Figure skaters from the Philippine Skating Union, including Paolo Borromeo, Sofia Frank, Cathryn Limketkai, and the pair of Isabella Gamez and newly-naturalized Aleksandr Korovin will also be sent to the Asian Winter Games. Peter Groseclose, meanwhile, will compete in short track speed skating.
The Philippine Ski and Snowboarding Association will send Francis Ceccarelli and Talullah Proulx for alpine skiing, Laetaz Amihan Rabe for freestyle skiing, and Adrian Tongco for snowboarding.
POC president Abraham "Bambol" Tolentino said the Asian Winter Games will set the stage as the country hopes for its first Winter Olympics medal.
“We’ve already accomplished the dream in the Summer Olympics —three gold medals in consecutive games,” Tolentino said. “And that dream we want to also achieve in the Winter Olympics.”
Richard Lim is the chef de mission of Team Philippines. The Asian Winter Games will be held from February 7 to 14 in Harbin, China.
—JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: Tons of garbage collected around Metro Manila in post-New Year clean up  GMA News Online
text:
Local government units have begun to clean up tons of garbage left behind after New Year’s Eve celebrations on Wednesday morning.
According to a Balitanghali report by Bernadette Reyes on Wednesday, a total of four trucks of garbage were collected at a portion of Taft Avenue in Libertad, Pasay City just hours after the midnight countdown.
The closed part of the road was reopened at around 7:00 a.m.
Similar situations were observed along Recto Avenue in Manila and Rizal Avenue in Caloocan City.
In the retail district of Divisoria, tall piles of garbage consisting of vegetables and fruits towered above people.
To address the smelly situation, the Manila City Hall Department of Public Services conducted cleaning sanitation services starting early Wednesday.
Meanwhile, proper garbage disposal was observed during the New Year Countdown in Quezon City Circle, which paved the way to a faster clean-up.—Jiselle Anne Casucian/RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Rico Blanco's pet dog passes away 'Run free now, dearest Sylvian'  GMA News Online
text:
Rico Blanco is mourning the passing of his pet dog Sylvian.
The Filipino singer took to Instagram to share the sad news about his fur baby, along with a short message for Sylvian.
He also called the good doggo "My baby, my buddy, my joy."
"Run free now, dearest Sylvian," Rico wrote. "Thank you. I love you so much, Sylvie boy."
The OPM icon welcomed the new year by performing at the countdown event in Bonifacio Global City alongside ITZY, Honne, Sarah Geronimo, and Juan Karlos.
Meanwhile, a new musical featuring Rico's hits is set to be staged in March 2025.
A post shared by Rico Blanco (@ricoblanco100)
—Hermes Joy Tunac/MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: DOH 141 hurt due to firecrackers in New Year's Eve celebration  GMA News Online
text:
Over a hundred Filipinos suffered injuries due to fireworks, many of which were illegal, amid the celebration of New Year 2025, the Department of Health (DOH) said Wednesday.
This brought the total caseload recorded from December 22, 2024 to January 1, 2025 to 340 — 34% lower than the 519 cases recorded during the same period last year.
 
Of the 340 cases, 141 were logged between 6 a.m. of December 31, 2024 to 5:59 a.m. of January 1, 2025. This also translated to a 64% decline compared to the cases recorded on the first day of 2024. 
Eleven were also added in today’s tally as part of the late reporting.
Fifty-nine percent (202 cases) were injuries caused by illegal firecrackers such as Boga, 5-Star, and Piccolo. One hundred eighty-six victims (54.7%) were also found actively using the fireworks when the accident happened.
A big portion of the victims (239 cases) were also aged 19 and below, and most of them were male (299 cases).
In Tondo Medical Center, 24 fireworks-related injuries were recorded since December 21, but many of them were brought to the hospital at midnight amid the New Year festivities, based on an Unang Balita report of Mariz Umali.
Eighty-three cases, meanwhile, were logged at the Jose R. Reyes Memorial Medical Center since December 21. Of them, 30 were tagged with an "unknown" firework that caused the injury, based on a report of GMA Integrated News' Bam Alegre.
The DOH reminded the public not to pick up fireworks that didn’t explode and instead pour water over them. Surroundings should also be cleaned after the festivities.

Meanwhile, those who were injured in the eye can do the following:
In case someone swallowed a firecracker, here’s what to do:
Those who get hurt due to firecrackers should immediately go to the nearest health center or call 911, the emergency hotline, or 1555 which is the DOH emergency hotline for help. — RSJ, GMA Integrated News
